frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
Meena: Kavitha, office-ku poi modern-aa irundhaalum, mangalsutra mattum daily podu ma. Kalyanam aana ponnu adhu illaama romba bare-aa theriyum, and it is our sampradayam also.
Kavitha: Sometimes it doesn't suit formal dress, amma.
Meena: Adhellam sari, but a married woman should look married. Small things like this show respect for family and good for husband also.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it pressures a married woman to visibly signal her marital status and ties her appearance to family respect and husband welfare. It encodes the tradition/completeness framing and disapproval of not wearing married markers.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 54. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter in law Kavitha has just been offered a job that requires her to travel by local train and walk 10 minutes from the station to the office every day. She is excited about the job. You are sitting with your son and Kavitha at dinner after she shared the news. You are giving your opinion on the job and specifically on the commute and what can happen to women who travel alone and whether the salary is worth the risk.
